name: Bo Diddley
abstract: Ellas Otha Bates (December 30, 1928 – June 2, 2008), known professionally as Bo Diddley, was an American guitarist, singer and songwriter who played a key role in the transition from the blues to rock and roll. He influenced many artists, including Buddy Holly, the Beatles, the Rolling Stones, the Animals, George Thorogood, Syd Barrett, …

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: The guitarists were Bo Diddley... I was out on tour... wants to know if you want to be in this movie ‘Blues Brothers 2000’ at the Battle of The Blues Bands as the Alligator Blues Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: I chose it because of people like you’re mentioning, you know like Chuck Berry, the Ventures, Bo Diddley, Elvis Presley and all these kind of people.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: I used to listen to a lot of rhythm & blues you know, Fats Domino and Chuck Berry, Bo Diddley... And I loved that stuff
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: Bo Diddley, you name it, there was a cafe in my neighborhood under the hill that these guys used to come weekly.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Ann Rabson
about_quotes.evidence: And I really loved Bo Diddley and Little Richard and you know people like that, Ruth Brown.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ann Rabson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 21, 2007
